name: SK6812 MINI-E
designNotes: Reverse-mount design -- LED faces down through hole in PCB for underglow or per-key lighting
Requires a cutout or window in the PCB for each LED
Preferred LED for Choc low-profile keyboards -- does not add height above PCB
Daisy-chain data: DOUT of one LED connects to DIN of the next
Add 100nF decoupling cap per LED (or at least every 3-4 LEDs)
3.3V logic from nRF52840 may need level shifter -- SK6812 expects 5V logic
At full white (all channels max), each LED draws ~15mA -- budget power accordingly
For 36-key split keyboard: 36 LEDs * 15mA = 540mA peak at full brightness
summary: Reverse-mount addressable RGB LED — shines through PCB for per-key and underglow lighting
features: Reverse-mount design shines through PCB cutout
Does not add height above PCB — ideal for low-profile builds
Low 15mA max current draw (vs 36mA for WS2812B)
Preferred LED for Choc low-profile keyboards
concerns: Requires PCB cutout/window for each LED
3.3V logic from nRF52840 may need level shifter for reliable data
36 LEDs at full brightness = 540mA peak — significant battery drain
Not suitable for top-mount per-key backlighting through keycaps
